title: BCSI Storage Location Inventory vs. Observed Disk Paths

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-011-3 R2 Part 2.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-011-3 R2 Part 2.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.58: BCSI Storage Location Inventory vs. Observed Disk Paths.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.58' running on index=inventory sourcetype=filesystem:scan, bcsi_approved_locations.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=inventory sourcetype=filesystem:scan earliest=-7d match(path,"(?i)one-line|relay|settings|cyber")
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup bcsi_approved_locations.csv host, path_prefix OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| stats values(path) as paths by host, bes_system
description: We compare discovered storage paths containing BCSI keywords against the approved storage location inventory.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes inventory and sourcetypes filesystem:scan, filesystem:scan`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Run constrained filesystem scans with legal approval. (2) Tune regex to reduce false positives. (3) Investigate unknown paths quickly. (4) Update inventory when validated. Indexes required: `index=inventory`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=filesystem:scan`, `sourcetype=filesystem:scan``; Lookups: `bcsi_approved_locations`, `bcsi_approved_locations.csv`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.
visualization: Table (unapproved paths), Single value (host count), Bar chart (by site)
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=inventory` `sourcetype=filesystem:scan`, `bcsi_approved_locations.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Run constrained filesystem scans with legal approval. (2) Tune regex to reduce false positives. (3) Investigate unknown paths quickly. (4) Update inventory when validated.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=inventory sourcetype=filesystem:scan earliest=-7d match(path,"(?i)one-line|relay|settings|cyber")
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup bcsi_approved_locations.csv host, path_prefix OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| stats values(path) as paths by host, bes_system
```

Understanding this SPL

**BCSI Storage Location Inventory vs. Observed Disk Paths (CIP-011-3 R2 Part 2.1)** — We compare discovered storage paths containing BCSI keywords against the approved storage location inventory.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=inventory` `sourcetype=filesystem:scan`, `bcsi_approved_locations.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: inventory; **sourcetype**: filesystem:scan. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=inventory, sourcetype=filesystem:scan, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, bes_system** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the official BES or cyber-asset list, the ESP edge firewall, and the same Windows or network data this search is built to mirror for that time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unapproved paths), Single value (host count), Bar chart (by site)
grandmaExplanation: We compare discovered storage paths containing BCSI keywords against the approved storage location inventory. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
